meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Master Project Management to Scale Your AI Apps | Student Guide
content.seo_description: Stop building basic apps. Learn expert project management strategies using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch professional-grade AI projects today.
content.answer_block: To break past basic app limitations, you must transition from coding to architecture. By implementing structured project management workflows, you can decompose complex features into modular tasks. Use Antigravity for automated scaffolding and Claude Code to maintain high-velocity iteration, ensuring your portfolio projects exhibit professional-grade engineering and scalability.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks grinding through tutorials, but your GitHub remains a graveyard of half-finished 'To-Do' lists and generic wrappers. You are stuck in the 'tutorial trap,' where the code works locally but falls apart the moment you try to scale into something meaningful. The gap between a student project and a production-ready application isn't just syntax; it is the discipline of project management.

Professional engineering is 20% writing code and 80% managing the complexity of your own ambition. By treating your code like a commercial product from day one, you shift from being a coder who follows instructions to an architect who builds systems. This guide will show you how to weaponize your workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to turn that next idea into a standout portfolio piece.

steps.title: Define the System Architecture
steps.description: Before writing code, map your dependencies and data flow. This prevents the 'spaghetti code' phase common in student projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Architect. Create a high-level technical specification document for a SaaS application that includes user authentication, a vector database integration, and a CRUD interface.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Create a README.md and a docs/architecture.md file based on the system requirements generated.'

steps.title: Bootstrap with Automated Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a production-ready folder structure, ensuring your project follows industry standards from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular project directory structure for a Next.js and Python FastAPI backend, including folder separation for services, controllers, and database models.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Initialize project directory structure and install essential dependencies for the backend.'

steps.title: Implement Iterative Feature Development
steps.description: Break complex features into micro-tasks. This keeps your momentum high and makes debugging significantly easier.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down the 'User Authentication' feature into five logical, testable sub-tasks with clear acceptance criteria.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Implement task 1: User sign-up flow using Supabase auth. Ensure error handling is robust.'

steps.title: Automate Documentation and Testing
steps.description: Don't let documentation lag behind your code. Use Claude Code to generate unit tests alongside your logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the primary API endpoints using Pytest. Focus on edge cases for input validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Write unit tests for the new API routes and run them to verify coverage.'

steps.title: Continuous Refactoring and Cleanup
steps.description: A project is never finished, only refined. Use professional tools to identify technical debt and optimize your codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for technical debt and suggest three refactoring tasks to improve performance and readability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Refactor the database connection pool logic based on the suggested improvements.'

pro_tips: Always commit after every sub-task completion to maintain a granular, reversible history.
Treat your README.md as the source of truth; if it isn't documented, it doesn't exist in your architecture.
Use Claude Code to generate 'ADRs' (Architecture Decision Records) whenever you choose a specific tech stack or library.
Limit your scope to one 'Core Feature' that works perfectly rather than five that are buggy.
Automate your feedback loop by having Claude Code summarize your commit history weekly to track your actual learning velocity.
content.next_steps: Once your first module is live, focus on deploying it to a cloud provider like Vercel or Railway. Documentation and deployment are the two metrics that separate a student from a professional developer.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop building 'toy' apps and start engineering real-world solutions. Don't let your ambition outpace your process; master the workflow, build the portfolio, and land the role. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
